model_summary: Initial gVAR-ready observation model focusing on six core cognitive-behavioral criteria (C01–C06) derived from the complaint, with eight modifiable predictors (P01–P08) spanning cognitive/metacognitive strategies, behavioral exposure, implementation planning, environmental feedback, self-compassion, social accountability, digital feedback, and sleep–circadian regulation. The design emphasizes momentary EMA capture (4 prompts/day) plus a minimal passive/diary stream to maximize within-person variance while containing redundancy. The predictor–criterion network is intentionally sparse and interpretable for subsequent graph-based VAR exploration, with explicit measurement modes, recall windows, and lag expectations. All measures are non-diagnostic and safety-oriented.”
variable_selection_notes: Rationale: The six criteria (C01–C06) capture memory-doubt, repeated checking, urge inhibition, lateness, social embarrassment, and self-criticism—central to the complaint and amenable to salutogenic interventions. The eight predictors (P01–P08) provide actionable levers across (1) metacognitive training, (2) ERP-like delay exposure, (3) implementation intentions, (4) environmental cueing (auto-lock), (5) self-compassion/self-talk, (6) accountability feedback, (7) digital measurement/feedback, and (8) sleep circadian regularity. To minimize collinearity, I selected one broad cognitive/metacognitive construct (memory confidence/urge monitoring), one behavioral strategy, a concrete environmental cue, a social support mechanism, a digital-feedback scaffold, and a physiological sleep regulator. Within-person variance is pursued via 4 daily EMA prompts and daily diary; passive streams include lock sensor signals and sleep data. Bias/uncertainty notes: C01–C06 come with moderate to high face validity but may correlate (e.g., embarrassment with lateness). Predictor feasibility is addressed through a mix of self-report and passive sensing; lag expectations reflect typical ERP/nonlinear learning dynamics (0–6h for immediate strategies; up to 12h for cumulative sleep effects).

momentary_ema.notes: 4 prompts/day x 14 days; jitter within blocks to reduce time-based biases.

daily_diary.notes: End-of-day reflection on day events, mood, and perceived checking.

passive_streams.notes: Door lock sensor events; sleep metrics from wearables; app engagement signals.

design_recommendations.gvar_readiness_notes: Pilot-ready design with basic EMA/passive signals; scalable to larger samples.
design_recommendations.variance_support_notes: Leverage 1–9 Likert scales for key constructs; avoid binary endpoints where possible.
design_recommendations.missingness_risk_notes: Anticipate partial compliance; include reminders and simple imputation guidance.

safety_notes: Not a diagnosis. No medication guidance. If any acute risk emerges, advise professional help and safety planning.

edges.notes: Memory-confidence training expected to decrease post-lock doubt.
Exit-planning reduces lateness risk per leaving episode.
Auto-lock reduces lateness due to checking.
Self-compassion practice reduces self-criticism after episodes.
Digital feedback supports memory confidence reductions in doubt.
System feedback dampens urge to re-check.
Sleep regularity supports punctual exit behavior.
Regular sleep reduces social discomfort after late departures.
Better sleep may reduce memory-doubt burden the next day.

source_variable.label: Failure to accept memory of locking

measurement.item_or_signal: Right after locking, rate your doubt about memory that you locked the door (1-9).

criteria.label: Compulsive checking repetitions
criteria.query_text_used: Compulsive checking repetitions | Operational item/signal: Since leaving, rate the urge to re-check the door (1-9). | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Assesses urge intensity during leaving episodes.

mapping.rationale: Variable C02 (Compulsive checking repetitions) aligns best with the 'door_lock_verification_repetition' facet of OCD-related checking. The measurement item 'Since leaving, rate the urge to re-check the door (1-9)' directly maps to door-lock verification repetitions. The EMA_self_report setup (4 prompts/day, recall since last prompt) captures momentary urges across leaving episodes, matching the variable's mechanism. Among candidates, door_lock_verification_repetition is the most specific and directly relevant to the given measurement focus, whereas other leaves imply different cues or broader checking behaviors.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since leaving, rate the urge to re-check the door (1-9).
measurement.notes: Assesses urge intensity during leaving episodes.

criteria.label: Difficulty resisting the urge to re-check
criteria.query_text_used: Difficulty resisting the urge to re-check | Operational item/signal: Right before acting on doubt, rate how difficult it was to resist re-checking (1-9). | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Inhibitory control during exit process.

mapping.rationale: C03 maps to difficulty resisting re-check in the moment, aligning with obsessive-compulsive checking constructs. Among candidates, repetitive_checking_behaviors (OBD/OC- related domain, checking subtype) is the most specific operational leaf capturing ongoing checking behavior propensity in the OCD-checking spectrum. The EMA_self_report, 1–9 scale, and 'Right before acting on doubt' context fit the item-focused urge-to-check resistance, making this the best leaf match among provided options.

source_variable.label: Difficulty resisting the urge to re-check

measurement.item_or_signal: Right before acting on doubt, rate how difficult it was to resist re-checking (1-9).

criteria.label: Time cost (lateness)
criteria.query_text_used: Time cost (lateness) | Operational item/signal: In the most recent leaving episode, how late were you due to checking? (1-9 scale; 1 = no lateness). | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Captures time-cost of checking behaviors.

mapping.rationale: Variable C04 assesses time-cost due to checking with lateness in a leaving episode, a punctuality/time-management construct. Among leaves, idx 3461 (NON-CLINICAL / AGE-GENERAL / psychological / temporal perspective / time management / meeting deadlines / adhering to deadlines) most specifically captures lateness-related punctuality in the context of deadlines and time management, aligning with the measurement emphasis on prompt leaving and deadline adherence. The mechanism rationale: EMA self-report 1-9 scale maps to degree of lateness; this leaf's semantics match time-cost of punctuality and adherence to deadlines; thus best fit for C04 within CRITERION ontology.

measurement.item_or_signal: In the most recent leaving episode, how late were you due to checking? (1-9 scale; 1 = no lateness).

criteria.label: Embarrassment in social context
criteria.query_text_used: Embarrassment in social context | Operational item/signal: Since the last leaving episode, rate your level of embarrassment or social discomfort (1-9). | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Social consequences of lateness/checking.

mapping.rationale: Variable: C05 Embarrassment in social context; measurement: EMA_self_report with 4 prompts/day; item: rate your level of embarrassment or social discomfort since the last leaving episode. Among candidates, the leaf 26414 (social_embarrassment_or_withdrawal_present) explicitly encodes embarrassment in social contexts and potential withdrawal, matching the label and intended measurement scenario. It is more specific to social embarrassment than performance-only or evaluative-threat references (e.g., fear of negative evaluation, cognitive_thoughts_of_embarrassment_during_performance). Although the leaf sits under DSM-5TR elimination disorders taxonomy, its content directly aligns with the Embarrassment in social context construct and supports momentary EMA assessment.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last leaving episode, rate your level of embarrassment or social discomfort (1-9).

criteria.label: Self-directed irritation/negative self-evaluation
criteria.query_text_used: Self-directed irritation/negative self-evaluation | Operational item/signal: After the checking episode, rate your level of irritation or negative self-evaluation (1-9). | Polarity: higher_is_worse | Notes: Captures self-criticism following checking.

mapping.rationale: Closest leaf: self_critical_rumination (idx=19031). The variable C06 is a momentary EMA item assessing self-directed irritation/negative self-evaluation after a checking episode. The leaf 'self_critical_rumination' explicitly encodes post-episode rumination about self-criticism/depressive self-views, aligning with the after-checking self-evaluation item (EMA_self_report, 1-9 scale, 4 prompts/day, recall since last prompt). This is more specific than general mood or embarrassment and matches the intended cognitive-affective mechanism (self-criticism after checking).

predictors.label: Metacognitive training: memory confidence
predictors.query_text_used: Metacognitive training: memory confidence | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Aims to improve confidence in memory for actions, reducing doubt after locking. | Intervention examples: Brief metacognitive prompts; cueing strategies; memory-confidence reflection. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | Today I completed memory-confidence metacognitive training exercise (1-9). | Prior ontology hint: [PSYCHO] / metacognitive_interventions / memory_confidence_training

mapping.rationale: Matches variable label: Metacognitive training: memory confidence. measurement.item explicitly references memory confidence recalibration within metacognitive interventions, and measurement.mode = EMA_self_report aligns with momentary self-report prompts. The closest, most specific operational leaf is cognitive_confidence_recalibration under PSYCHO / metacognitive_interventions / (cognitive_confidence_recalibration). Mechanism-wise, recalibrating confidence in memory for actions reduces doubt after locking, which fits the described predictor purpose. source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Brief metacognitive prompts; cueing strategies; memory-confidence reflection.

measurement.item_or_signal: Today I completed memory-confidence metacognitive training exercise (1-9).

feasibility.notes: Moderate burden; feasible with app-based prompts.

predictors.label: ERP with Delay to Check
predictors.query_text_used: ERP with Delay to Check | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Delay-based exposure and response prevention reduce habitual checking. | Intervention examples: ERP-like practice with controlled delays; harmless exposure tasks. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | Today I engaged in an ERP-like delay-to-check exercise (1-9). | Prior ontology hint: [PSYCHO] / cognitive_behavioral_therapies / exposure_and_inhibitory_learning

mapping.rationale: The variable P02 is described as ERP with Delay to Check, a delay-based exposure and urge-prevention task. The best-matching ontology leaf is at PSYCHO / cognitive_behavioral_therapies / exposure_and_inhibitory_learning / response_prevention_and_urge_work / delay_and_alternatives / delay_and_distract_urge_skills (ERP-like delay, with strategies to distract or delay urges). This leaf directly captures delay-based response prevention and urge management, aligning with the measurement item: Today I engaged in an ERP-like delay-to-check exercise (1-9), assessed via EMA_self_report (4 prompts/day, 1-9 scale, since_last_prompt). Thus it is the most specific operational embedding for the variable within the provided candidates.

source_variable.label: ERP with Delay to Check
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Delay-based exposure and response prevention reduce habitual checking.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: ERP-like practice with controlled delays; harmless exposure tasks.

measurement.item_or_signal: Today I engaged in an ERP-like delay-to-check exercise (1-9).

feasibility.notes: Requires careful app scheduling to avoid burden.

predictors.label: Implementation intentions for exit
predictors.query_text_used: Implementation intentions for exit | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: If-then planning facilitates cue-based action initiation, reducing forgotten exit steps and possibly lateness. | Intervention examples: Pre-planned exit routines; if-then rules anchored to leaving cues. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | Today I used my exit plan before leaving (1-9). | Prior ontology hint: [PSYCHO] / change_process_and_therapeutic_phases / behavioral_solutions / implementation_intentions

mapping.rationale: Best-matching PREDICTOR leaf: idx=2284 corresponds to PSYCHO / change_process_and_therapeutic_phases / behavior_change_theories_and_modern_protocol_arcs / habit_and_dual_process_solutions / *implementation_intentions_ifthen_design_routine. The variable label is 'Implementation intentions for exit' and the measurement is EMA_self_report ('Today I used my exit plan before leaving'), which directly maps to an if-then implementation intention design. This leaf is the most specific operational construct among candidates that captures 'implementation intentions' in a design-routine form applicable to exit planning, aligning with the mechanism rationale (cue-based action initiation via if-then planning) and the EMA measurement item.

source_variable.label: Implementation intentions for exit
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: If-then planning facilitates cue-based action initiation, reducing forgotten exit steps and possibly lateness.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Pre-planned exit routines; if-then rules anchored to leaving cues.

measurement.item_or_signal: Today I used my exit plan before leaving (1-9).

feasibility.notes: Depends on participant adherence to planning steps.

predictors.label: Door lock status indicator and auto-lock
predictors.query_text_used: Door lock status indicator and auto-lock | Domain: SOCIAL | Mechanism: External sensor feedback and automatic locking reduce reliance on memory and timing, potentially lowering lateness (C04) and memory-doubt (C01). | Intervention examples: Install/verify auto-lock; real-time indicator feedback. | Measurement: passive_phone_sensor | Daily door lock events and auto-lock status (count per day). | Prior ontology hint: [SOCIAL] / Environmental_exposures_and_built_environment_optimization / Door_lock_signals

mapping.rationale: Best match is leaf with bedroom_door_lock_function_check (SOCIAL domain in path sensing door_lock signals). This aligns with var_id P04: Door lock status indicator and auto-lock, which is an external environmental cue captured via sensors. The measurement item (daily door lock events and auto-lock status) corresponds to the door lock signals concept, and mechanism-wise, external sensor feedback reduces reliance on memory/timing, potentially lowering lateness (C04) and memory-doubt (C01). Although the candidate's full ontology path shows a BIO breadcrumb, the content (door lock signals as an environmental cue) is the closest fit among leaves to P04’s intended functional role and measurement modality (passive sensing via environmental cues).

source_variable.label: Door lock status indicator and auto-lock
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: External sensor feedback and automatic locking reduce reliance on memory and timing, potentially lowering lateness (C04) and memory-doubt (C01).

measurement.item_or_signal: Daily door lock events and auto-lock status (count per day).

feasibility.notes: Depends on device integration; feasible with minimal burden.

predictors.label: Self-compassion and self-talk training
predictors.query_text_used: Self-compassion and self-talk training | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Enhances adaptive self-regulation and reduces self-criticism (C06). | Intervention examples: Brief self-compassion prompts; compassionate self-talk scripts. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | Today I practiced self-compassion or compassionate self-talk (1-9). | Prior ontology hint: [PSYCHO] / core_psychological_skills_toolkits / Emotion_Regulation_and_Affective_Mastery

mapping.rationale: Best-matching leaf: PSYCHO / core_psychological_skills_toolkits / compassionate_self_relating_and_inner_support / compassionate_cognition / reframes_and_voice / supportive_inner_voice_practice. This maps directly to self-compassion and inner self-talk training: it focuses on compassionate self-relating and inner-supportive self-talk, which aligns with the variable label 'Self-compassion and self-talk training' and the EMA item 'Today I practiced self-compassion or compassionate self-talk (1-9)'. The measurement mode EMA_self_report and 4 prompts/day fit the within-person EMA design. Mechanistically, supportive inner voice practice enhances adaptive self-regulation and reduces self-criticism (C06) within emotion regulation/affective mastery, consistent with the predictor’s intended function. Pseudoprofile context supports this mapping as a lever across self-compassion and self-talk implementations.

predictors.label: Digital measurement and feedback system
predictors.query_text_used: Digital measurement and feedback system | Domain: BIOxPSYCHO | Mechanism: Continuous digital feedback and monitoring can optimize engagement with other predictors. | Intervention examples: Usage of an EMA app with real-time feedback dashboards. | Measurement: passive_phone_sensor | Daily engagement with the feedback system (minutes of active use or prompts completed). | Prior ontology hint: [PSYCHO] / Digital_and_Tech_Enabled_Solutions / Digital_Tools_and_Supports

mapping.rationale: Best-matching leaf: activity_engagement_tracking under PSYCHO / measurement_based_care_and_feedback / measurement_and_feedback_systems. The variable Digital measurement and feedback system (P07) has measurement.mode = passive_phone_sensor and measurement.item_or_signal = Daily engagement with the feedback system (minutes of active use or prompts completed), with measurement.recall_window = past_24_hours and measurement.sampling_per_day = 1. This maps directly to an engagement-tracking construct for a digital feedback system measured via app engagement minutes/day, aligning with the predictor's role in digital feedback scaffolding and EMA design.

source_variable.label: Digital measurement and feedback system
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Continuous digital feedback and monitoring can optimize engagement with other predictors.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Usage of an EMA app with real-time feedback dashboards.

measurement.item_or_signal: Daily engagement with the feedback system (minutes of active use or prompts completed).

feasibility.notes: Relies on participant device compatibility; generally feasible.

predictors.label: Sleep circadian regularization
predictors.query_text_used: Sleep circadian regularization | Domain: BIO | Mechanism: Regular sleep-wake schedules support cognitive control and reduce variability across the day, potentially reducing C04–lateness and C05 embarrassment. | Intervention examples: Consistent bed/wake times; light exposure management. | Measurement: passive_phone_sensor | Daily sleep onset time, wake time, and duration; variability across days. | Prior ontology hint: [BIO] / Sleep_Circadian_and_Restoration / Circadian_Entrainment

mapping.rationale: P08 maps to Sleep circadian regularization. The most specific predictor leaf aligning with this is 'consistent_morning_anchor_routine' (BIO / sleep_circadian_and_restoration / circadian_entrainment / light_cues / consistent_morning_anchor_routine). This operationalizes circadian entrainment through a concrete morning routine and light cues, directly supporting regular bed/wake times and reducing day-to-day sleep variability, which matches the measurement of daily sleep onset, wake, and duration via passive daily aggregates. Other candidates are either broader (social_rhythm_stabilization) or more narrowly focused on evening light (late_evening_screen_reduction) or specific lighting devices, making 6324 the most specific and appropriate operational construct for Sleep circadian regularization as a predictor.

source_variable.label: Sleep circadian regularization
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Regular sleep-wake schedules support cognitive control and reduce variability across the day, potentially reducing C04–lateness and C05 embarrassment.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Consistent bed/wake times; light exposure management.

measurement.item_or_signal: Daily sleep onset time, wake time, and duration; variability across days.
measurement.notes: Sleep data used to compute regularity index.

feasibility.notes: Depends on device compatibility; generally feasible with wearables.
